subject: Ecology
title: Soil Bacteria
description: A micro-lesson that makes Soil Bacteria usable.

hook.text: Bacteria are the smallest organisms in soil—but they run the biggest chemical reactions.

buildup.text: They fix nitrogen, decompose organic matter, and transform minerals into plant food.

discovery.text: Different bacterial communities dominate different soils—pH, moisture, and carbon type matter.

twist.text: Soil bacteria produce antibiotics. Streptomycin, used to treat tuberculosis, came from soil.

climax.text: Most soil bacteria are unknown to science. Every gram may hold species never yet described.

punchline.text: The pharmacy and the factory are both in the dirt.

quiz.question: Where was the antibiotic streptomycin discovered?
